creator: yklaxds@gmail.com
text: The default shell used by the FreeBSD installer to create users should be adjusted to csh or tcsh instead of sh.The default shell for FreeBSD users creating programs is sh. The default shell for root users is csh. This results in inconsistencies in the shells for root and normal users, which leads to inconsistencies in how environment variables are configured for both. This leads to inconsistent configuration of input methods and the desktop i18n language. I believe that the default new user shell in the FreeBSD installer's user creation program should be adjusted to csh or tcsh to maintain consistency with the root user's environment variable configuration. Adjusting the default new user shell created to csh or tcsh would also be more in line with FreeBSD's development goals.
